title: Shinjis thoughts on Kaworus death
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's thoughts on Kaworu's death             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Sep 19, 2013 2:43 am Kendrix wrote: Oh, another interesting bit i stumbled over in that same googling session is that, apparently, Shinji was supposed to have lines after Kaworu's death (In Q) , but Ogata couldn't bring herself to say them, so Anno impromptu decided to cut em all, probably for realism. Noe I'm kinda curious as to what they would have been.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Sep 19, 2013 3:51 am Well, it depends at which moment exactly he was supposed to say these lines : just after Kaworu's death, during Mari's rousing speech, when Asuka confronted him just after opening his entry plug, or after when she was kicking his back? If it's just after Kawrou's head goes boom and/or during Mari's speech, I see it as a "I'm sorry" repeated like an insane mantra. If it's later with Asuka, it could be Shinji repeating something like "I didn't wanted that." "I'm sorry." "Why did you took it[the DSS Choker] from me, it should have been me." over and over and over again... But I think that it would have completely freaked out Asuka, and make the moment she manhandle him really cruel. Because the way I see it, Asuka in the end didn't realized yet just how severe Shinji's mental condition is, she think that he's just being childish and pouting like a brat who refuses to talk after being caught doing a mistake, while he's actually in what looks like a grave case of psychotic catatonia. Let's see if how she react after Shinji don't talk after many days in FINAL. But they must be really heartwreching lines if Ogata couldn't bring herself to say them, her interview showed that she's a very sensible person and that what happens to Shinji in Q seems to have really shaken her, it must have been a really grueling job for her! Anno you fucking sadist!

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Thu Sep 19, 2013 9:12 pm ... is there actually any proof that that's true?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Sep 19, 2013 11:20 pm Th3Marauder wrote: ... is there actually any proof that that's true?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Sep 20, 2013 12:33 am It's also her JOB as an actor to give her own input into the character & what they're feeling to help make it a more dynamic & realized performance. Directors for the most part are not actors, they know how they want a performance to come across but not how to get there emotionally. They're storytellers. More often then not the greatest directors trust their actors & their instincts. It's what allows a strongly written character on the page to become a richer & more nuanced character on the screen. The director gives the character the emotional storyline & the lines, but it's the actor who brings those pages to life. A director who doesn't trust his/her actors is not a director... it's someone who doesn't care. Film - whether it be live action or animation - is a collaborative effort where everyone has to trust in the expertise & skill of everyone else for the ship to work. The actors & crew need to trust that Anno will direct the film into being the story he wants to tell. On Anno's behalf, he has to trust that his crew can animate what he's asking & that the actors can make the emotions he's going after come alive. Also, from the sound of it it's more likely less " Ogata told Anno to shove it " & more that she was in the physical & emotional mindset of the character in order to deliver the best possible performance & it didn't feel right or justifiable for Shinji to say anything. She could've forced the lines out but they wouldn't come across as natural. Anno, being a director who has worked in both animation & live action, probably recognized that if the actor doesn't feel right saying the lines then most likely the dialogue - which might work gangbusters on the page - doesn't work when spoken in the scenario that the story was presenting & that the lines are superfluous & should be cut. Frankly, if this situation is true then I think Ogata and Anno made the right choice. It's heart-wrenching to see Shinji just shut down once Kaworu dies but it makes sense emotionally & logically that he would do so. Having Shinji say anything - even if it's just him muttering "I'm sorry, I'm sorry" or brokenly responding to Asuka's jabs - could come across as Shinji having a little strength left in him. P.S. For the record, some people can say what they want about 3.0 being "a bad movie" or whatever, but I would say it should be a given that Megumi Ogata has given her strongest & most nuanced performances as Shinji Ikari to date in You Can (Not) Redo.

[]:             Quote Post by Sunface » Fri Sep 20, 2013 12:37 am ^ This. As someone who has worked in the service industry for all of my adult life I have done a lot of things I have not wanted to do. But if we're being paid, we do it. If it is true it would have to be something extremely out there. Like maybe 'Why did you lie to me? You told me I could fix this...' Only something like that would make it even remotely plausible. But still no. Edit: I got ninja'd trying to respond to guy nacks. The next post does have some good points, but I just can't see that if Anno wanted these lines in there that somebody else can say 'nope', and that's that. Maybe it was how 'rushed' the schedule was, but I certainly would have said the lines the director gave me.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Sep 20, 2013 3:12 am Sunface wrote: I just can't see that if Anno wanted these lines in there that somebody else can say 'nope', and that's that.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Sep 20, 2013 12:04 pm Actors are a vital part of the creative process, they're not mere tools of the director. The actor's job, after all, is to become that person, which would include knowing what they would or wouldn't say, or what would or would not sound natural as part of their performance. Unless the director is Stanley Kubrick, but I doubt Anno made Ogata do fifty-five takes without sleep. Auteur theory much?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 20, 2013 2:28 pm pwhodges wrote: The usual story about the last line of EoE suggests that change is possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Sep 20, 2013 8:28 pm Ogata not "bringing herself to say [the deleted lines]" sounds like she didn't want to because she couldn't summon the proper emotion that Anno wanted....which, as an actress, she should be able to do. I'm all for collaboration between actors and directors, but I feel like Ogata should have at least tried to give Anno a few takes. But we're all operating off an article I havent been able to read as of yet, so this might all be for naught.

[]:             Quote Post by Th3Marauder » Sat Sep 21, 2013 12:46 am We're crucifying Shinji's voice actor and we still have no proof this is even real!

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Sep 21, 2013 9:32 am Th3Marauder wrote: We're crucifying Shinji's voice actor and we still have no proof this is even real!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Sep 21, 2013 10:39 am ^ Fumbling around with various language translators I found online, it seems as though Anno was in agreement with the changes suggested to the script, and Ogata even jokes about she and Anno being at "400% sync ratio" with one another on the decision. So, I guess this was creativity and not fear that motivated this change. Seriously, do you know how many people "have trouble with" certain lines simply because they don't seem to work when spoken? I'm surprised the actors in The Room or Star Wars The Phantom Menace were able to keep a straight face while simply regurgitating the words they took in from the page back into the director's ear. (Star Wars especially. Young and up-and-coming actor Harrison Ford ad libs a line making one of the best moments in the whole saga in The Empire Strikes Back for director Irvin Kershner, and over 20 years later more established and accomplished actors are forced to take what they're given from George Lucas? Sheesh!) Also, do you know how many actors want to have their "Oscar Moment" (or whatever award they have in Japan) when it comes to chewing the scenery with melodrama? Seems as though if the discarded line was truly wrenching enough, she would have pounced on it rather than question it. Maybe actors actually like spring-boarding from a competent director's ideas off into something slightly more crazy.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Sep 21, 2013 11:41 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: ^ Fumbling around with various language translators I found online, it seems as though Anno was in agreement with the changes suggested to the script, and Ogata even jokes about she and Anno being at "400% sync ratio" with one another on the decision. So, I guess this was creativity and not fear that motivated this change. Seriously, do you know how many people "have trouble with" certain lines simply because they don't seem to work when spoken? I'm surprised the actors in The Room or Star Wars The Phantom Menace were able to keep a straight face while simply regurgitating the words they took in from the page back into the director's ear. (Star Wars especially. Young and up-and-coming actor Harrison Ford ad libs a line making one of the best moments in the whole saga in The Empire Strikes Back for director Irvin Kershner, and over 20 years later more established and accomplished actors are forced to take what they're given from George Lucas? Sheesh!) Also, do you know how many actors want to have their "Oscar Moment" (or whatever award they have in Japan) when it comes to chewing the scenery with melodrama? Seems as though if the discarded line was truly wrenching enough, she would have pounced on it rather than question it. Maybe actors actually like spring-boarding from a competent director's ideas off into something slightly more crazy.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Sep 21, 2013 2:41 pm Perhaps Anno thought that the silence would simply convey the same thing the words would have, even better. Like, "Oh wait, this is an even better Idea than the one I had. Let's used." Changing your mind is not a sighn of weakness or cluelessness; It's being receptive to criticism and willing to improve, and to admit that there may be better ways of doing something than those you envisioned. "It should have been me!" or anything like that would certainly fit the depiction of it in the OST bonus track "Famously"... Also, I don't see how words/mad rambling would have made Asuka's treatment any harsher - He's tightly curled into a ball and completely unresponsive. It's rather obvious what kind of state he's in. Words MAY have changed the fan response (of Shinji, not Asuka), tho, though in what way is hard to gauge without having the words. I hope the 3.0 CR will have it, just for curiosity value. Then again, we might still hear those same mad ramblings in 4.0 once the initial shock wears off a bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Sep 21, 2013 3:01 pm Being harsh to somebody who's incoherent to snap them out of it is a pretty long standing storytelling device.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Sep 21, 2013 4:00 pm Either way, from looking at the actual text/notice, it looks less like "Ogata couldn't summon enough emotion", but that she was too overcome with emotion to keep on her text... See also her discriptions of having been thrown into despair by this movie. Shinji is in a horribloe situation; If ppl in this very forum have had such strong reactions to thinking too hard about what it must be like, imagine what it must be like for the chick whose job it is to imagine what it's like, and get it across to us. Honestly, she deserves a freakin oscar for her work in this movie; From the moment I first watched, I was impressed by howmuch she outdid herself.

[]:             Quote Post by Lord ikari shinji » Mon Sep 23, 2013 10:19 pm Shinji is fuckin mentally destory Hopefully Anno gives Asuka a comfirming role toward Shinji, otherwise she gonna make him worst off, mari please help them

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Thu Oct 03, 2013 2:28 am Not to be disgusting, but does Kaworu's head explode or does his entire body explode into red LCL like the other Angel's (besides ZeuRei/ Zeurial+Rei) bodies do? In the blood splatter, there's no bone, teeth or brain fragments anywhere and also there's no rainbow, presumably because there's no more Covenants to be made to Lilith, who's been dead for 14 years. Maybe Anno was pushed by censorship to make his death less graphic, but I doubt it.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Oct 03, 2013 3:28 am Just his head, if you look closely at the bottom of the blood splatter you can see his legs. It's darkly ironic : in NGE his whole body gets destroy minus the head(who fall in the lake of LCL) and in NTE it's his head which is pulverized, leaving only the rest of the body! As for the lack of rainbow, I don't think it has anything to do with the covenant with Lilith, since the NS Code 04B and Mark.09 left a rainbow when they died, and when the 12th Angel died it left one too, but Lilith is long since dead. I think the absence of a rainbow is a sign that Kaworu isn't completely dead, maybe his soul transferred inside EVA-13(after all it's an ADAM), which would explain the brief shot of Kaworu falling in a light in the background when Shinji was ejected. And Kaworu did promised to Shinji that they will meet again!

Noe I'm kinda curious as to what they w
